title: https://github.com/ShunsukeHayashi/agent-skill-bus FUGUE/Kernelに必要か、批判的吟味せよ
summary_text: Selective adoption is likely overrated in this case. - `agent-skill-bus` as implemented is not just “ideas”; it brings a storage/control model (`JSONL queue`, file-lock TTL, whole-file status rewrites) that is weak for concurrent orchestration ([queue.js#L4](https://github.com/ShunsukeHayashi/agent-skill-bus/blob/master/src/queue.js#L4), [queue.js#L155](https://github.com/ShunsukeHayashi/agent-skill-bus/blob/master/src/queue.js#L155), [queue.js#L266](https://github.com/ShunsukeHayashi/agent-skill-bus/blob/master/src/queue.js#L266)). - The “closed-loop self-improving” claim is ahead of code reality: metrics and drift detection exist, but diagnose/propose/evaluate/apply are not automated ([README.md#L21](https://github.com/ShunsukeHayashi/agent-skill-bus/blob/master/README.md#L21), [self-improve.js#L109](https://github.com/ShunsukeHayashi/agent-skill-bus/blob/master/src/self-improve.js#L109), [self-improve.js#L142](https://github.com/ShunsukeHayashi/agent-skill-bus/blob/master/src/self-improve.js#L142)). - Knowledge watching is checker-hook based, not an autonomous watcher engine ([knowledge-watcher.js#L74](https://github.com/ShunsukeHayashi/agent-skill-bus/blob/master/src/knowledge-watcher.js#L74)). - FUGUE and Kernel already have overlapping telemetry/audit surfaces, so partial import risks duplicate ledgers and policy drift ([run-local-orchestration.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/scripts/local/run-local-orchestration.sh#L1210), [kernel-lane-bridge.mjs](/Users/masayuki/Dev/kernel-orchestration-tools/kernel-lane-bridge.mjs#L14), [always-on-kernel-architecture.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/kernel-orchestration-tools/always-on-kernel-architecture.md#L48)). - Test signal is thin for reliability: top-level repo listing exposes no dedicated `tests/` suite from this run (`.github`, `src`, `skills`, `examples`, etc.), so concurrency/failure semantics look under-verified. Where a more ambitious path could work: - If you adopt it as a **single hardened event substrate** (not piecemeal), with transactional queueing, lease-based locks, idempotency keys, retries/DLQ, schema versioning, and explicit FUGUE-vs-Kernel sovereignty boundaries. - Without that hardening, the middle path (“selective but broad”) is the highest-risk option: maximum integration cost, minimum systemic payoff.
